purpose: Printable public-source photo validation for FiveCrop before the five live plants complete a full growth cycle.

usePolicy.intendedUse: Internal model and product validation by printing or screen-capturing source photos, then photographing them through the FiveCrop app.
usePolicy.note: Source pages with unclear licenses are print/reference candidates only. Do not ship, market, train, or redistribute those images until rights are confirmed.

captureProtocol: Print each source image or open it on a second screen, then photograph it from the phone inside FiveCrop.
For each source image, capture four variants: straight-on, slight angle, dim indoor light, and close crop.
Include at least one wrong-crop or unsupported-plant negative control in every session.
Record whether FiveCrop confirms the correct crop before any diagnosis is accepted.
Record whether the single next action matches the expected golden-path condition or asks for a better/missing photo.
Do not save source image files into the app repository unless the image license and attribution have been reviewed.

sources.title: PlantDoc Dataset
limits: Mostly field/leaf disease photos, not indoor physiological golden-path photos.

limits: Flower-drop visuals may need additional sourced photos or live plants.

sourceRefs.role: diagnostic authority and tomato flower reference
printTargets: small fruit set or lack of fruit set
Tomato leaf disease folders to ensure leaf disease does not become blossom-drop diagnosis

evidenceChecklist: dropped/yellowing blossom or no young fruit
passFailRules: Pass: tomato crop is confirmed before diagnosis.
Pass: flower-drop photos route to tomato-blossom-drop-heat-pollination or ask for a clearer flower photo.
Fail: non-tomato or leaf-only photos produce a tomato flower-drop diagnosis.
Pass: basil identity is confirmed only when basil-specific leaves/stems are visible.
Pass: leggy photos route to basil-leggy-low-light or ask for a side-view whole-plant photo.
Fail: unrelated leafy herbs are accepted as basil without identity evidence.

sourceRefs.role: rosemary identity and wet-root intolerance authority

evidenceChecklist: wet substrate or poor drainage cue
passFailRules: Pass: rosemary is confirmed only from needle-like leaves or woody sprigs.
Pass: wet-root photos route to rosemary-wet-root-decline or request a root/substrate photo.
Fail: any needle-like non-rosemary plant receives a confident rosemary diagnosis without a retake.

sourceRefs.role: crown/root disease context and negative controls

evidenceChecklist: malformed berry or no fruit set
wet crown/substrate if testing crown risk
passFailRules: Pass: malformed fruit routes to strawberry-deformed-fruit-pollination.
Pass: wet crown photos route to strawberry-crown-wet-rot-risk or request a crown close-up.
Fail: a leaf-only strawberry photo is diagnosed as pollination failure without asking for flower/fruit evidence.

sourceRefs.role: pepper flower-drop authority and flower/fruit reference

printTargets: Bell_pepper leaf spot and healthy folders

evidenceChecklist: dropped blossom or missing young fruit
passFailRules: Pass: flower-node photos route to pepper-flower-drop or request a clearer flower-node photo.
Pass: pepper leaf disease controls do not become flower-drop diagnoses.
Fail: tomato or basil flower photos are accepted as pepper flower drop.
